content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why They're Probably Wrong)
What State Laws Say About Deposit Deadlines & Deductions
TikTok & Reel Hooks: Turn Your Frustration Into Action
The 60-Second Fix: Send a Demand Letter Without a Lawyer
When to Escalate: Small Claims Court & Tenant Advocacy
key_points: Hook 1 — POV: 'You deep-cleaned the apartment but your landlord just sent a $400 cleaning bill...'
Hook 2 — Storytime: 'My landlord won't give me my deposit back, so I spent 60 seconds doing THIS.'
Hook 3 — Myth-bust: 'If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 21-30 days (depending on your state), they might owe you double or triple.'
Hook 4 — Comparison: 'Normal wear and tear vs. damage. Your landlord is betting you don't know the difference. Let's fix that.'
Script 1 — [Text on screen] 'Day 45. Still no deposit. So I went to Deposits-Back.com...' [Cut to letter sent] '...got my money back in 7 days.'
Most states limit deductions to actual damages and unpaid rent; 'cleaning fees' for ordinary move-out grime are often illegal.
A professionally drafted demand letter citing state statutes is the fastest way to pressure a landlord before filing in small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com generates a state-specific security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee—no sign-up, no subscription.
output.cta: Stop waiting. Send your security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription, just results.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, deducting from, or refusing to return security deposits, and who are actively searching for fast, affordable legal remedies without hiring an attorney.
